approvedDivergences.legacyBehavior: Stale TypeScript assertions expected a 42m route.
approvedDivergences.targetBehavior: The current demo_1.edges.json route total of 46m is authoritative.

approvedDivergences.legacyBehavior: Expo iOS attitude.yaw radians were passed through as heading degrees when magnetometer heading was unavailable.
approvedDivergences.targetBehavior: The Flutter iOS adapter converts yaw radians to normalized degrees before publishing fallback heading.

pdr.description: A normal quiet-to-peak batch moves one step along the route.
No samples preserves position and previous heading.
A single peak has no preceding quiet sample.
An extreme acceleration spike is rejected as shake.
NaN acceleration records the existing JavaScript non-finite behavior.
Positive infinity is classified as an extreme shake spike.
The transient batch sorts timestamps before step detection.
Only stale samples after a sensor interruption are dropped.
Forty high-frequency samples are capped to the newest thirty-two.
Two sparse but fresh samples still form a valid quiet-to-peak batch.
A future sample is dropped before analysis.
A new peak inside the minimum step interval is rejected.
Startup lock suppresses movement without suppressing heading or step diagnostics.
A confirmed strong backward step moves opposite the route heading.

tolerances.specialNumbers: NaN and infinities must match by classification
